summary: Short customer testimonial from Val Delana praising Cavallo Sport and Simple hoof boots and the Simple Ride Mask across all her horses.
Carole Herder's reflection on a visit to Oakham Castle in England, where decorated horseshoes are displayed, used to argue against nailing metal shoes onto horses' feet.
Carole Herder essay on the emotional difficulty horse owners face when changing or leaving their farrier in order to transition their horse from shod to barefoot.
Customer testimonial from Jill Fender describing how she repurposed Cavallo Simple Boots as soaking boots by adding vinegar to treat hooves.
Customer travel testimonial about a trail-riding holiday in Italy, contrasting the experience with concern over poorly shod horses and advocating barefoot/booted hooves.
Carole Herder essay on the evolution and domestication of horses over thousands of years, arguing against the archaic practice of nailing metal shoes and for keeping horses barefoot.
First-hand account of a two-day barefoot trimming course in the UK run by Paddock Paradise Europe (AANHCP), covering hoof history, the wild hoof model, and hands-on cadaver trimming.
Carole Herder article on the plight of wild horses in North America and Australian Brumbies, covering population control, drought, and habitat pressures.
Light-hearted news piece about two Jack Russell terriers, Freddie and Percy, who ride a Shetland pony named Daisy at a farm in Gloucestershire, England.
Carole Herder guide to growing safe herbs for horses, covering dandelion, chamomile, mint, calendula, echinacea, and willow and their medicinal or nutritional benefits.
Carole Herder article on keeping horses fit through movement, with practical ideas like spacing food and water, alleyways, and Jaime Jackson's Paddock Paradise design.
Carole Herder event recap of Cavallo's sponsorship of the North American Police Equestrian Championships (NAPEC) in Richmond, Virginia, describing the mounted-unit competition.
